name: Genetic Engineering Platforms
what: Tools to design and edit biological systems.
how: CRISPR and DNA synthesis program cells like software.

name: Engineered Therapeutics
what: Cell and gene therapies from synthetic biology.
how: Reprogrammed cells treat disease at the source.

name: Biomanufacturing
what: Fermentation-based production of chemicals and materials.
how: Engineered microbes brew sustainable products.

name: De Novo Protein Design Milestones
use: Designing proteins with specific functions and properties.
what: Advances in computational methods for creating custom-designed proteins.
how: Enables the development of new drugs, materials, and industrial enzymes.

name: CRISPR 3.0 (Base Editing, Prime Editing)
use: More precise genome modifications without double-strand breaks.
what: Next-generation CRISPR tools like base editing and prime editing allow for targeted gene modifications.
how: Improves the accuracy and efficiency of genetic engineering.

name: Synthetic Ecosystems for Carbon Capture
use: Engineered microorganisms to capture and convert carbon dioxide.
what: Synthetic ecosystems can be designed to capture CO2 and convert it into useful products.
how: Develops microbial communities that can sequester and utilize CO2 for various applications.

name: CRISPR 3.0 (Base Editing and Prime Editing)
what: CRISPR 3.0 technologies offer more precise gene editing capabilities with fewer off-target effects.
how: These technologies expand the potential for genetic therapies.

name: Synthetic Ecosystems for Carbon Capture, Engineered Organisms for Plastic Digestion
what: Creating synthetic ecosystems and engineered organisms to address carbon capture and plastic pollution.
how: Developing biodegradable materials and microorganisms that can break down plastics and sequester CO2 from the atmosphere.

name: De Novo Protein Design
use: Creating entirely new proteins with specific functions.
what: Designing and synthesizing novel protein structures.
how: Applying de novo design to biotech applications.
companies: University of California at San Diego

name: Prime Editing
what: Precision genome editing without double-strand DNA breaks.
how: Fusion of Cas9 nickase and reverse transcriptase.

name: Synthetic Organisms for Space Exploration
use: Creating self-replicating organisms that can thrive in extraterrestrial environments.
what: Designing microorganisms capable of surviving and thriving on other planets or moons.
how: Using synthetic biology to create robust, adaptable life forms that can perform specific tasks in space.

name: Synthetic Life Forms for Bioremediation
use: Creating organisms that can clean up environmental pollutants and toxins.
what: Designing microorganisms capable of breaking down harmful substances in the environment.
how: Using synthetic biology to create robust, pollutant-degrading life forms.

name: Engineered Organisms for Plastic Digestion
what: Biological solutions to break down and recycle plastic waste.
how: Genetically modified microorganisms capable of degrading polymers.

name: De Novo Protein Design for Drug Discovery
what: Advancements in de novo protein design enable the creation of novel proteins with specific therapeutic functions.
how: These proteins can be used to develop new treatments for diseases like cancer and autoimmune disorders.

name: Precision Fermentation at City Scale
what: Large-scale production of bio-products using advanced biotech.
how: Optimized fermentation processes for cost and efficiency.

name: Biofuel Production
what: Using engineered microorganisms to produce biofuels from agricultural waste and other renewable resources.
how: Optimizing metabolic pathways in bacteria for efficient biofuel production.

name: CRISPR 3.0 Base Editing
what: Advanced CRISPR tools for precise gene editing and therapeutic applications.
how: Improving accuracy and efficiency of gene editing technologies.

name: Longevity Medicine with CRISPR 3.0
what: Enables precise editing of the human genome.
how: Extends lifespan and improves healthspan through targeted gene therapies.

name: Synthetic Carbon Capture Organisms
what: Engineered microorganisms designed for hyper-efficient CO2 fixation and conversion into stable biopolymers.
how: Optimization of the Calvin cycle and introduction of synthetic metabolic pathways.

name: Synthetic Carbon-Sinking Ecosystems
what: Engineered microbial mats designed for hyper-accelerated carbon sequestration.
how: Optimizing the RuBisCO enzyme for higher efficiency and mineralizing results into calcium carbonate.

name: Xenobiology
what: Life forms using non-canonical amino acids or nucleic acids.
how: Engineered ribosomes and polymerases capable of processing unnatural building blocks.

name: Protein Diffusion Models
what: AI models that generate novel protein structures from scratch.
how: Using diffusion processes to reverse noise into viable 3D protein folds.

name: De Novo Protein Design for Climate Solutions
what: Aims to create entirely new proteins with specific functions.
how: Addresses environmental challenges through synthetic biology.
companies: University of California, Berkeley · Synthetic Genomics
